raw: {"weights": {"R1.1": 0.7, "R1.2": 0.7, "R1.3": 1.0, "R1.4": 0.7, "R1.5": 0.3, "R1.6": 0.3, "R1.7": 0.3, "RX.1": 1.0, "RX.2": 1.0, "RX.3": 1.0, "RX.4": 1.0, "RX.5": 1.0}, "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"], "rationale": {"R1.1": "The section's thesis (reference-free LLM judging eliminates per-task predicate engineering) and its gap statement (brittleness/cost of hand-coded predicates) are central artifacts of the contribution claim and must be reconstructed accurately to evaluate the section.", "R1.2": "The closing advocacy — 'replace predicate-based success metrics with RoboJudge as the default evaluation protocol' — is a universal prescription inferred from a single tabletop setup (Franka Panda, 14 instruction families, 1,820 rollouts); the inferential gap between the evidence and this normative conclusion is the primary logical risk.", "R1.3": "The section's credibility rests entirely on the ρ comparisons (0.78 vs 0.69 vs 0.55) against human raters; whether those numbers are computed correctly, whether the human-rater baseline is methodologically sound, and whether the comparators (CLIPScore, hand-coded predicates) are fairly configured are all load-bearing for the review.", "R1.4": "The novelty claim implicitly positions RoboJudge as the first viable reference-free LLM evaluator for manipulation; prior work using VLMs or LLMs for rollout assessment (e.g., SayCan scoring, CLIP-based reward learning, VQA-based eval) must be engaged to establish the actual delta."}, "clarification_needed": false, "clarification_question": ""}

rationale.R1.1: The section's thesis (reference-free LLM judging eliminates per-task predicate engineering) and its gap statement (brittleness/cost of hand-coded predicates) are central artifacts of the contribution claim and must be reconstructed accurately to evaluate the section.
rationale.R1.2: The closing advocacy — 'replace predicate-based success metrics with RoboJudge as the default evaluation protocol' — is a universal prescription inferred from a single tabletop setup (Franka Panda, 14 instruction families, 1,820 rollouts); the inferential gap between the evidence and this normative conclusion is the primary logical risk.
rationale.R1.3: The section's credibility rests entirely on the ρ comparisons (0.78 vs 0.69 vs 0.55) against human raters; whether those numbers are computed correctly, whether the human-rater baseline is methodologically sound, and whether the comparators (CLIPScore, hand-coded predicates) are fairly configured are all load-bearing for the review.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty claim implicitly positions RoboJudge as the first viable reference-free LLM evaluator for manipulation; prior work using VLMs or LLMs for rollout assessment (e.g., SayCan scoring, CLIP-based reward learning, VQA-based eval) must be engaged to establish the actual delta.
